DISCOVERY 301, HEARING ON 2- MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF OLGA DASHDORJS FORM AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES TO DEFENDANT MARTIN EBERT, SET ONE, AND FOR SANCTIONS. THE MATTER IS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF OLGA DASHDORJ'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO HER FORM AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED IN PART. SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT TO DEPT. 301 A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-03-2026 Set For Trial On Aug-24-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff Olga Dashdorjs Request For Admissions To Defendant Martin Ebert, Set One, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF OLGA DASHDORJ's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff Olga Dashdorjs Request For Admissions To Defendant Martin Ebert, Set One, And For Sanctions.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Ryan Kent, Esq., (415) 307-1552, specially appearing for attorney Roger S. Reynolds, for Plaintiff.
Sydney Baba, Esq., of Phillips, Spallas & Angstadt LLP, (415) 278-9400, for Defendant.

1-The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Olga Dashdorj's motion to compel further responses to her requests for admission is granted in part and denied in part.

Dashdorj's meet and confer efforts were sufficient. The parties narrowed their disputes but some disputes remained. Dashdorj was not required to continue her efforts where the parties' positions were not likely to continue to change.

RFA Nos. 1, 2, 8, 9, 11, 12, 16, 41, 42, 43, 44, 45, 46, 47: These RFAs ask defendant Martin Ebert to admit or deny certain propositions about the accident giving rise to this case. Ebert states that he has made a reasonably diligent inquiry but that he cannot admit or deny because he has insufficient personal knowledge in light of his memory loss; he also explains why various documents provided him to by Dashdorj do not enable him to admit or deny. In some instances Ebert claims a lack of knowledge. Upon review of each of the RFA responses, no further response is ordered. A party must respond completely and nonevasively to proper RFAs, but Ebert has answered these in full. A motion to compel further responses does not lie to require a party to change the content of an RFA answer. Rather, the propounding party may seek the cost of proof after trial when a party refuses to admit propositions that are sufficiently clear. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2033.420.)

RFA Nos. 5, 7, 10, 14, 15, 18, 31, 33, 36, 38, 50: Ebert's objections lack merit; the RFAs are sufficiently clear that they can and should be answered. Ebert shall amend and provide a substantive response.

RFA No. 6, 17, 19, 20, 21, 22, 23, 24, 32, 37, 48, 49: Ebert's objections have merit; no further response is ordered.

Ebert shall make the further responses ordered herein within two weeks and shall amend his responses to Form Interrogatory 17.1 where appropriate where further responses have been made to RFAs.

Both parties seek sanctions. The court concludes that both sides' positions were partially justified and does not award sanctions.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit to Dept. 301 a proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff Olga Dashdorjs Form And Special Interrogatories To Defendant Martin Ebert, Set One, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF OLGA DASHDORJ's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff Olga Dashdorjs Form And Special Interrogatories To Defendant Martin Ebert, Set One, And For Sanctions.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Ryan Kent, Esq., (415) 307-1552, specially appearing for attorney Roger S. Reynolds, for Plaintiff.
Sydney Baba, Esq., of Phillips, Spallas & Angstadt LLP, (415) 278-9400, for Defendant.

The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Olga Dashdorj's motion to compel further responses to her form and special interrogatories is granted in part.

Many of the interrogatories at issue on this motion ask about Martin Ebert's speed. Ebert takes the position that he does not need to respond with data from his Garmin device because that would be expert opinion. This is incorrect. Information is not shielded from ordinary discovery because it is also might be the subject of expert opinion. Moreover, a Garmin device is used by cyclists to track their rides, including their speeds, without resort to experts to understand and use.

Special Interrogatory Nos. 1, 2, 3, 29, 30, 31, 32: Ebert's objections lack merit, including his objection that the interrogatory calls for expert opinion or is premature.

Special Interrogatory Nos. 7, 8, 9; Form Interrogatory Nos. 12.1: Ebert's response is sufficient and no further response is ordered. A party can be compelled to give a complete and nonevasive answer to a discovery request, but it cannot be compelled to give a substantively different answer.

Form Interrogatory No. 12.2: Ebert shall amend. If Ebert has not conducted any witness interviews, he must so state. If he has conducted some, and maintains that work product protects his selection of whom to interview, he must provide more information to enable Dashdorj to evaluate the work product claim.

Form Interrogatory No. 15.1: Ebert's objection that the question is premature lacks merit; he shall amend.

Form Interrogatory Nos. 16.1, 16.2, 16.3, 16.4, 16.5, 16.6, : No further response is ordered at this time. In light of the early stage of the case, the court sustains Ebert's objections. Nothing in this order prevents Dashdorj from re-serving these form interrogatories later in the case.

Form Interrogatory Nos. 20.1, 20.3, 20.4, 20.8, 20.9: Ebert's objections that these interrogatories cannot be used in a bicycle-pedestrian accident lack merit. Ebert shall amend.

Dashdorj's meet and confer efforts were adequate; the court agrees that the parties were at impasse at the time the motion was filed.

Ebert shall amend where ordered within two weeks.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit to Dept. 301 a proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)
